title: Vibrant Spicy Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A nutty and juicy Tunisian dish featuring chicken stock cube and ferment-kimchi, perfect for a midnight craving, this gluten-free-friendly tagine is a delight to the senses.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chicken stock cubes, ground cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add the chopped ferment-kimchi to the pot and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
